items.headline: Equinix data center expansion permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center building received its permit at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard.

items.headline: Chick-fil-A demo and addition permitted on Blossom Hill Road
items.summary: Chick-fil-A received a permit for demolition and expansion of its location at 1162 Blossom Hill Road, with a budget of $809,557.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument artwork agreement approved
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo, located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will be a public installation at the park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget approved
items.summary: City Council took action on the 2026-2027 budget and annual assessment for the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District. The district funds improvements in the area east of Tully Road.

items.headline: Cupertino General Plan Health and Safety Element Update
items.summary: The City Council reviewed an update to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan (2015–2040). The agenda does not specify what changes or new policies are being proposed.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent city manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution to appoint a permanent city manager. This fills what may have been a vacant or interim position.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates water shortage plans and urban water strategy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These documents set Sunnyvale's water strategy and outline how the city will manage supply during droughts.

items.headline: Spring cycle of community event grants opens for applications
items.summary: The City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications for the spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood projects.
